text: **Anna:** Hello!
**Anna:** Washington DC has many {beautiful|美しい/見事な} parks.
**Anna:** In fact, this {park|公園} reminds me of my home very far away
**Marsha:** Anna, here's your coffee.
**Anna:** Thanks, Marcia.
**Marsha:** What's wrong?
**Anna:** I'm thinking about my family.
**Anna:** I am feeling {homesick|郷愁を感じる/家が恋しい}
**Marsha:** You want to talk about it?
**Anna:** Sure.
**Anna:** I have some {photos|写真}.
**Marsha:** Yes, yes you do.
**Anna:** Photos really help.
**Anna:** This is my mother and this is my father.
**Marsha:** They are {rodeo|ロデオ/牛馬競技} clowns.
**Marsha:** What do rodeo clowns do?
**Anna:** They make {jokes|冗談/笑い話} at a rodeo.
**Anna:** People laugh.
**Marsha:** That that's very different.
**Marsha:** Who is that woman in the picture?
**Anna:** That is my Aunt Lavender.
**Anna:** She is my mom's sister.
**Marsha:** She loves {gardening|園芸} and makes spoons.
**Marsha:** She makes spoons?
**Anna:** Of course.
**Marsha:** That, too, is very different.
**Anna:** Oh, this is my Uncle John.
**Anna:** He is my father's brother.
**Marsha:** What does Uncle John do?
**Anna:** He's a {chicken|鶏/ニワトリ} farmer and makes guitars.
**Anna:** He's {awesome|素晴らしい/すごい|/ˈɔːsəm/|He is an awesome player.}
**Anna:** And I'm his favorite {niece|甥/姪|/niːs/|My niece is very smart.}
**Marsha:** Who are they?
**Anna:** They are my {cousins|いとこ|/ˈkʌzn/|My cousins live in Tokyo.}
**Anna:** They are my Uncle John's {daughter|娘|/ˈdɔːtər/|My daughter is five years old.} and son.
**Marsha:** What do they do?
**Anna:** They {raise|育てる/飼育する|/reɪz/|We raise chickens on our farm.} {sheep|羊|/ʃiːp/|Sheep live on the farm.} and make {sweaters|セーター|/ˈswetərz/|She wears warm sweaters in winter.}
**Marsha:** Yeah, that's not a {surprise|驚き/意外|/sərˈpraɪz/|It was a big surprise.}
**Marsha:** Thanks for showing me your family photos.
**Marsha:** Your family is very {different|異なる/違う|/ˈdɪfərənt/|They have different ideas.}
**Anna:** I do feel {better|より良い|/ˈbetər/|I feel better today.}
**Anna:** Thanks for listening.
**Anna:** I have many more photos.
**Marsha:** Yeah?
**Marsha:** Yeah, you do.
**Anna:** Washington, D.
**Anna:** C.
**Anna:** is my new {home|家/故郷|/hoʊm/|Home is where family is.}, but I like {remembering|思い出す|/rɪˈmembərɪŋ/|I'm remembering my childhood.} my old home too.
**Anna:** This is a family tree.
**Anna:** Anna tells Marcia about her parents.
**Anna:** Her mother and father are {rodeo|ロデオ|/ˈroʊdioʊ/|Rodeo is a cowboy sport.} {clowns|道化師|/klaʊnz/|Clowns make people laugh.}
**Anna:** Her father's parents are from Italy.
**Anna:** These grandparents speak {Italian|イタリア語|/ɪˈtæliən/|Italian food is delicious.}
**Anna:** Anna's mother's parents live in California
**Anna:** These grandparents have a farm and {raise|育てる/飼育する|/reɪz/|We raise chickens on our farm.} horses.
**Anna:** Anna's mother's sister is Aunt Lavender.
**Anna:** She loves {gardening|園芸|/ˈɡɑːrdənɪŋ/|Gardening is her hobby.}
**Anna:** And his father has a brother.
**Anna:** His name is John.
**Marsha:** Uncle John makes {guitars|ギター|/ɡɪˈtɑːrz/|He plays guitars in a band.}
**Anna:** Uncle John has a {daughter|娘|/ˈdɔːtər/|My daughter is very smart.} and a {son|息子|/sʌn/|My son plays soccer.}
**Marsha:** They are Anna's {cousins|いとこ|/ˈkʌzənz/|My cousins live in Tokyo.}
**Anna:** They {raise|育てる/飼育する|/reɪz/|We raise chickens on our farm.} {sheep|羊|/ʃiːp/|Sheep live in the countryside.}
**Anna:** Anna's brother has two {children|子供たち|/ˈtʃɪldrən/|Children love to play.}
**Anna:** They are Anna's {niece|姪|/niːs/|My niece is very kind.} and {nephew|甥|/ˈnɛfjuː/|My nephew is a student.}
